data.title: Gotbit founder sentenced to 8 months in prison for manipulating crypto market, team banned from operating for 5 years
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Gotbit founder and CEO Aleksei Andriunin was sentenced to eight months in prison by the U.S. District Court in Massachusetts on Friday for manipulating the crypto market and carrying out millions of dollars of wash trading. He will be subject to one year of supervised release after serving his sentence.
The prosecutor pointed out that Andriunin and the Gotbit team provided fake trading volume manufacturing services for multiple crypto projects from 2018 to 2024, forged on-chain activity through code and multi-account operations, helped the project to be listed on CoinMarketCap and attracted large exchanges to go online. Gotbit received tens of millions of dollars in compensation for this.
In addition, Gotbit was also sentenced to five years of probation, during which time it was not allowed to continue to operate related businesses. The prosecutor said Andriunin was arrested in Portugal in October 2024, extradited to the United States in February 2025, and signed a plea agreement in March this year.

data.title: Gotbit CEO Sentenced for Crypto Market Manipulation
data.description:
According to Odaily, Aleksei Andriunin, founder and CEO of Gotbit, has been sentenced to eight months in prison by a Massachusetts district court for manipulating the cryptocurrency market and conducting millions of dollars in wash trades. Following his imprisonment, Andriunin will be subject to one year of supervised release.

Prosecutors revealed that from 2018 to 2024, Andriunin and the Gotbit team provided services to multiple crypto projects, creating false trading volumes through code and multi-account operations to fabricate on-chain activity. This strategy helped projects gain visibility on CoinMarketCap and secure listings on major exchanges, earning Gotbit tens of millions of dollars.

Additionally, Gotbit has been sentenced to five years of probation, during which it is prohibited from continuing related operations. Prosecutors stated that Andriunin was arrested in Portugal in October 2024 and extradited to the United States in February 2025, where he signed a plea agreement in March.

data.title: JPMorgan Increases Target Prices for Bitcoin Mining Companies Amid Rising Profits
data.description: According to PANews, JPMorgan has raised the target prices for several Bitcoin mining companies, including CleanSpark, Riot Platforms, and MARA Holdings, reflecting the increase in Bitcoin prices and improved mining profits. The target price for CleanSpark (CLSK) has been increased from $12 to $14, Riot Platforms (RIOT) from $13 to $14, and MARA Holdings to $19. The bank maintains an 'overweight' rating for CleanSpark, IREN, and Riot Platforms, while keeping a 'neutral' rating for Cipher and MARA. Analysts have adjusted the expected Bitcoin spot price upward by 24% and increased the estimated network hash rate by 9%.

data.title: MicroStrategy Executives Acquire $1.3 Million in Preferred Shares
data.description: According to Odaily, several insiders at MicroStrategy have recently purchased approximately $1.3 million worth of the company's preferred shares. This acquisition highlights the confidence of the company's leadership in its financial strategies and future prospects.

data.title: Cardano Founder Proposes Treasury Allocation to Boost Stablecoin Presence
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Cardano founder Charles Hoskinson has suggested using $100 million worth of ADA from the treasury to convert into Bitcoin and stablecoins, including USDM and USDA. This proposal aims to enhance the proportion of stablecoins within the Cardano ecosystem and its decentralized finance (DeFi) sector.

Hoskinson assured that this move would not negatively impact the ADA market and countered concerns about liquidity. Currently, stablecoins on the Cardano blockchain account for only about 10% of the total value locked (TVL), which is significantly lower compared to Solana's stablecoin ecosystem.

This proposal contrasts with the views of Cardano Foundation CEO Frederik Gregaard, who previously emphasized that TVL is not a critical metric for evaluating the ecosystem's success.
